{'text': "
Before this study you answered an interview question.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.

Answer: I am a white, able-bodied, male in my mid-thirties who was born and raised in central Florida. I grew up in a rural farming community in central Florida with a population of just over 250. I was the youngest in my family and only had sisters. I was very sheltered as a child and very unsure of myself. Having sisters taught me a lot about myself because the family functioned like a democracy. I felt voiceless in a patriarchal household. The family didn’t have enough money for any physical activities so I wasn’t physically active as a child. I loved dinosaurs as a kid and my hobbies were fishing, science, and the natural world around me. As a teenager, I was fairly uncool. I had braces as a teen and very little experience with romance. My family did not have a great understanding of mental health so I just felt depressed and alone most of the time. I was enrolled in a vocational agriculture program in high school and learned how to be a Dairy Farmer. I got into 4-H and took farm management as an advanced program. We visited other farms in the area to learn about new innovations. I learned how to operate and repair milking machines, herd identification, and how to take milk samples to measure quality. After graduating, I was allowed to enroll in a dual enrollment program and attended community college while still in high school. This enabled me to earn an Associates of Arts degree by the time I graduated high school. My family moved to Western Pennsylvania in search of better employment opportunities because my mother’s health deteriorated and she was no longer able to work. I gained experience in customer service and early leadership through my work as a cell phone customer service representative while I was in college. I am deeply interested in space exploration and aerospace engineering. Currently, I am an aerospace engineer at NASA and I do quite a bit of work with crews preparing for mission. I am heavily involved in popular media channels that have discussions about topics in politics, science, philosophy, and engineering. Finally, I enjoy hiking, rock climbing, and spending time outdoors.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: I am 31 years old. I am originally from Windsor but I am currently living in London. I think the most defining moment for me was when I was adopted from an orphanage in Latvia.

When I first found out I was adopted I couldn’t really handle the knowledge. Later on though I came to see it as a blessing. Adoption showed me that I can be loved and provided for no matter what.

They always had my back and I can remember many amazing family events where I felt truly loved and taken care of. It took some time but eventually I would regard my parents as my parents and I still think about what a difference they made in my life.

I think of those moments when I look at the song choices in my set list as a musical director because I try to find and play the songs that remind me of the time I felt most loved or most special.

Music is an amazing thing. When I play it I feel safe, warm, and surrounded by love. I can be happy and sad, euphoric and sad, energetic, and lazy all to the tune of a few well chosen notes.

However music for me isn’t just a way for me to escape or to express myself. Music for me as a musical director is a communication tool. Its how I lead the dancers to dance. It’s how I get them thinking and feeling together in harmony.

Of course music can be used by God too. Music can lift you up when you are feeling lost, alone, and forgotten.

Anytime when you are ready, whether in excitement or in despair just listen and you can always find that you are never really alone.

Before this study you answered an interview question.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: I am 31 years old. I am originally from Windsor but I am currently living in London. I think the most defining moment for me was when I was adopted from an orphanage in Latvia.

When I first found out I was adopted I couldn’t really handle the knowledge. Later on though I came to see it as a blessing. Adoption showed me that I can be loved and provided for no matter what.

They always had my back and I can remember many amazing family events where I felt truly loved and taken care of. It took some time but eventually I would regard my parents as my parents and I still think about what a difference they made in my life.

I think of those moments when I look at the song choices in my set list as a musical director because I try to find and play the songs that remind me of the time I felt most loved or most special.

Music is an amazing thing. When I play it I feel safe, warm, and surrounded by love. I can be happy and sad, euphoric and sad, energetic, and lazy all to the tune of a few well chosen notes.

However music for me isn’t just a way for me to escape or to express myself. Music for me as a musical director is a communication tool. Its how I lead the dancers to dance. It’s how I get them thinking and feeling together in harmony.

Of course music can be used by God too. Music can lift you up when you are feeling lost, alone, and forgotten.

Anytime when you are ready, whether in excitement or in despair just listen and you can always find that you are never really alone.
